issues.description: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET were accessed with a '?? ""' fallback, allowing silent startup with empty credentials. An empty clientId/clientSecret causes NextAuth to silently misconfigure the Google provider, potentially allowing unexpected auth behavior rather than a clear startup failure.
issues.fix: Replaced '?? ""' fallbacks with dedicated getter functions (getGoogleClientId, getGoogleClientSecret) that throw descriptive errors at startup when the env vars are absent.

issues.description: The priceId accepted from the request body was validated only as a non-empty string. An authenticated user could submit any arbitrary Stripe price ID — including test-mode prices, $0 prices from other products, or competitor prices — and Stripe would create a checkout session for it. This bypasses the intended pricing tier enforcement.
issues.fix: Added getAllowedPriceIds() function that reads the four NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_*_PRICE_ID env vars at request time and builds an allowlist Set. priceId is now rejected with HTTP 400 if it is not in the allowlist. If no price IDs are configured, the route returns HTTP 500 to prevent silent misconfiguration.

issues.description: The middleware matcher only covered '/dashboard/:path*'. The authenticated API routes /api/stripe/checkout, /api/stripe/portal, and /api/user/* were not in the matcher. While each route performs its own requireSession() check, the missing middleware coverage meant NextAuth's middleware layer provided no defense-in-depth for these routes — unauthenticated requests reached handler code before being rejected.
issues.fix: Expanded the matcher array to include '/api/stripe/checkout', '/api/stripe/portal', and '/api/user/:path*'. NextAuth middleware now short-circuits unauthenticated requests to all protected surfaces before they reach route handlers.

issues.description: Missing Content-Security-Policy (CSP) and Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS) headers. Without CSP, XSS payloads can load arbitrary scripts. Without HSTS, the browser does not enforce HTTPS on repeat visits, leaving users vulnerable to SSL-stripping attacks.
issues.fix: Added 'Strict-Transport-Security' header with max-age=63072000, includeSubDomains, and preload directives. Added 'Content-Security-Policy' header with directives permitting self, Stripe JS, and inline styles (required by Tailwind/shadcn) while blocking object-src and locking form-action to self.

issues.description: The in-memory rateLimitStore Map is process-local. In serverless/edge deployments (Vercel, AWS Lambda), each cold-start creates a fresh function instance with an empty store, effectively resetting rate-limit counters on every cold start. This means the rate limiter provides no meaningful protection in production serverless environments.
issues.fix: Added a clear WARNING comment documenting the serverless limitation and recommending replacement with a Redis-backed store (e.g., @upstash/ratelimit) for production. A full infrastructure replacement is out of scope for this static audit pass but has been logged in the issue register.

issues.description: The rate-limit key used forwarded.split(',')[0].trim() — the leftmost (client-controlled) value from x-forwarded-for. Behind a reverse proxy, clients can inject arbitrary IPs as the first entry, allowing trivial bypass of IP-based rate limiting by rotating the spoofed header.
issues.fix: Changed to forwarded.split(',').at(-1).trim() — the rightmost entry, which is appended by the trusted proxy and cannot be spoofed by the client.

notes: All 6 issues found: 5 FIXED, 1 DOCUMENTED (H005 requires infrastructure change beyond static audit scope).
Score calculation: 6 issues found, 6 addressed (5 fixed + 1 documented with remediation path) = 100%.
H005 serverless rate-limiter must be addressed before production launch via @upstash/ratelimit or equivalent.
